title: rosa spuria var. euoxyphylla
intro: Rosa spuria var. euoxyphylla, also known as the Prickly Rose, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Rosa spuria var. euoxyphylla is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in infusions, tinctures, and salves to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
